metric_value: Create de facto standard SQL interface between data mining tools and RDBMS using SQL extensions
notes: Core strategic objective of the Tandem ORDM initiative

metric_value: Five data mining partners integrated into ORDM: Angoss (KnowledgeSEEKER); Data Distilleries (Data Surveyor); Magnify (PATTERN:Detect/Profit); NeoVista (Decision Series); Syllogic (DM Tool/MP)
notes: One-stop data mining via multi-vendor algorithm integration

metric_value: Data-manipulation functions integrated into RDBMS; enterprise executes against data directly in warehouse; no flat-file extract required
notes: Eliminates mandatory extract-transform-load step for data mining

metric_value: Scales from SMP to MPP as data mining demands grow; runs on ServerWare/NT or Himalaya servers
notes: Platform-agnostic scalability from departmental to enterprise scale

metric_value: Determine what data will likely return best results — precedes algorithm execution
notes: Aberdeen: majority of design resources consumed before algorithm runs

metric_value: Data scrubbing — removing data and field inconsistencies
notes: Critical pre-processing step that ORDM architecture facilitates by sharing warehouse tools

metric_value: Coding the data — formatting data to work best with selected algorithm
notes: Algorithm-specific data preparation step

metric_value: Algorithm execution and results review; re-tuning model and re-running algorithm iteratively
notes: Iterative nature of data mining recognized as key characteristic

metric_value: Three initial solutions packages: credit card marketing; micromerchandising (market basket analysis); claims analysis
notes: Finance/retail/insurance verticals targeted based on Tandem's existing customer base

metric_value: Object Relational Data Mining architecture available Q3 1997 on ServerWare/NT and Himalaya; credit card/micromerchandising/claims packages available without SQL extensions immediately
notes: Announced February 1997 with Q3 1997 general availability target

metric_name: ORDM as enterprise data mining standard
metric_value: Tandem's ORDM architecture will become an end-to-end enterprise weapon for competitive advantage; Aberdeen believes enterprises can look to it as the standard solution
notes: Aberdeen endorsement of ORDM as go-to architecture for enterprise data mining

metric_value: Compaq acquired Tandem in 1997; ORDM architecture was deprioritized; de facto standard SQL data mining interface goal not achieved; Oracle/Microsoft/Informix did not adopt the standard
notes: Compaq $3B acquisition ended Tandem's independent standards push

metric_value: In-database analytics became dominant approach in modern MPP systems (Teradata, Greenplum, Vertica, Snowflake, BigQuery); Tandem's architectural vision proven correct
notes: The architecture concept succeeded even as the specific Tandem implementation did not

metric_value: Directional Consulting designs and builds data mining environment within 90 days; charter is knowledge transfer not prolonged engagement; leaves software toolbox
notes: Differentiated consulting model: fixed-duration engagement with IP transfer

metric_value: 98.8% scalability across 112 CPUs in TPC-C benchmark — cited from Aberdeen 1994 Product Viewpoint on Himalaya K10000
notes: Referenced to establish Tandem's parallel computing credibility

metric_value: Parallel-scalable architecture; 5 integrated data mining partner algorithms; solutions packages with vertical templates; consulting services — end-to-end capability
notes: Four-part competitive differentiation identified by Aberdeen

metric_value: $360M+ annual software sales cited; key accounts in finance/insurance/retail/telecoms
notes: Establishes Tandem's financial scale and market position pre-acquisition

metric_name: Aberdeen assessment of ORDM value proposition
metric_value: ORDM increases overall reliability of supplier code; saves data mining suppliers from writing redundant code; encourages more value-added development
notes: Aberdeen's positive assessment of ORDM's ecosystem effects

metric_value: ORDM on ServerWare/NT will enable cost-sensitive scale from Windows NT to Himalaya in cost-sensitive increments
notes: Prediction that Windows NT-based entry point would drive enterprise data mining adoption

metric_value: ServerWare discontinued after Compaq acquisition; NT-based product line did not survive consolidation
notes: Compaq focused on Himalaya line; ServerWare dropped

metric_value: Tandem Computers acquired by Compaq for approximately $3B in 1997; became NonStop server division
notes: Acquisition announced June 1997; completed same year
